payload.title: node-forge X.509 certs reject ERR_OSSL_ASN1_ILLEGAL_PADDING under Node 24's OpenSSL
payload.description:
Repro: 'CI: Test (Node 24)' job; test/revocation.test.js:61 throws ERR_OSSL_ASN1_ILLEGAL_PADDING when constructing 'new X509Certificate(leaf.certPem)' from a cert that node-forge (src/ca.js issueLeafCert) just signed. Same code passes on Node 20, 22, and 26 (locally tested). Specifically: 'error:068000DD:asn1 encoding routines::illegal padding'.

Suspected: Node 24 ships an OpenSSL build (~3.5) with stricter ASN.1 validation that catches an encoding quirk in forge's cert output that Node 20/22/26 (different OpenSSL builds) accept. node-forge 1.4.0 is already the latest.

Workarounds to consider:
- Round-trip the forge-signed cert through Node's native crypto to re-canonicalize before storing.
- Replace forge cert signing with Node's experimental key/cert APIs (Node 22+ added some).
- Patch forge's INTEGER encoding for the cert serial / validity fields.

CI matrix temporarily drops Node 24 to keep main green. Re-add once this is fixed.
